text: There are still too many brigands for you to fight in here.  You need to find some way to block one of the doors.
speaker: narrator

text: Your bug doesn't have any hands.  This makes throwing anything out of the question.
speaker: narrator

text: Looks like all you can do is wriggle.
speaker: narrator

text: Better concentrate on the game.
speaker: narrator

text: The bright smell of fresh herbs mingles with the aroma of wood smoke as you near a hut by the side of the road.
speaker: narrator

text: How embarrassing, to get this far and then fail to see the trip rope.  How did they get that Antwerp up there?
speaker: narrator

text: You're too impatient to rest right now.
speaker: narrator

text: For a moment there, you thought you saw something.  But nothing is there now.
speaker: narrator

text: You've taken out the brigand, but... oh, no!  Here comes another one!
speaker: narrator

text:
Congratulations!
You have discovered the first bug in this game.
speaker: narrator

text: Wearing out your welcome.
speaker: narrator

text: One thrust...and that's it???
speaker: narrator

text: You put the seed into your pack and climb down.
speaker: narrator

text: Getting into the castle the hard way.
speaker: narrator

text: Oh-h-h-h-h-h-h!
speaker: narrator

text: Watch your step!
speaker: narrator

text: You do not have any daggers.
speaker: narrator

text: You're the Fall Guy again.
speaker: narrator

text: Have I been here before?
speaker: narrator

text: Greedy Greedy Greedy .
speaker: narrator

text: He who hesitates...
speaker: narrator

text: A travelling man.
speaker: narrator

text: WHATA TROLL!
speaker: narrator

text: There is nobody to open the portcullis.
speaker: narrator

text: Leave the climbing to those who know how.
speaker: narrator

text: You're not in a good place to climb.
speaker: narrator

text: The guards won't allow you to do that.
speaker: narrator

text: The castle looms over the gatehouse and looks rather impressive.
speaker: narrator

text: The towers stand out above the castle walls.
speaker: narrator

text: The sky is clear except for a few clouds.
speaker: narrator

text: The grass seems to grow well here.
speaker: narrator

text: You see the road leading past the Healer's house.
speaker: narrator

text: You see the forest.
speaker: narrator

text: "Come in.  Come in.  You have been expected.  Baron von Spielburg awaits you in the castle."
speaker: guard

text: "Are you speaking to me?"
speaker: guard

text: "Who's there? Come out and show yourself!"
speaker: guard

text: "Well?  Enter the castle grounds!"
speaker: guard

text: Once more the guard raises the portcullis.
speaker: narrator

text: The guard, sensing something is awry, raises the portcullis.
speaker: narrator

text: You're not a quick learner.  Now you known why the grass grows so well around the portcullis.
speaker: narrator

text: That smarts.  Maybe you should move a little more quickly.
speaker: narrator

text: Had you been healthier, you probably could have survived that fall.  In your weakened condition, however, you succumbed to your injuries.
speaker: narrator

text: Experience is the best teacher.
speaker: narrator

text: Practice makes perfect.
speaker: narrator

text: Try, try again etc...
speaker: narrator

text: Take a break.  It's Mueller time.
speaker: narrator

text: The gatehouse is more difficult to climb than it first appeared.
speaker: narrator

text: Remember what happened to Humpty Dumpty.
speaker: narrator

text: After climbing down the wall you says to yourself, "Self", you says, "that wasn't too bad."
speaker: narrator

text: You can't see much of the gatekeeper over the battlements.
speaker: narrator

text: "As you can see, the gate is open."
speaker: guard

text: "I will not open for a faceless voice. Come out where I can see you."
speaker: guard

text: "Very well."
speaker: guard

text: "If you wish."
speaker: guard

text: "As you can see, the gate is closed."
speaker: guard

text: Don't keep everybody waiting with idle talk.
speaker: narrator

text: "Then go, before I close the portcullis."
speaker: guard

text: "Sorry.  I wasn't listening.  Could you repeat youself?"
speaker: guard

text: "OK."
speaker: guard

text: "My name is Karl."
speaker: guard

text: "That's me. I guard the portcullis day after day."
speaker: guard

text: "The portcullis is the metal grate that bars the entrance to the castle yard.
speaker: guard

text: "This is the Castle of the Baron Stefan von Spielburg, Lord of this Valley."
speaker: guard

text: "My Lord is busy organizing his troops."
speaker: guard

text: "My Lord sees no one since the loss of his son and daughter."
speaker: guard

text: "Ten years ago the Baron's daughter, Elsa, was stolen away by magic.  Five years back, the Baronet Barnard von Spielburg went on a hunt from which he did not return."
speaker: guard

text: "This was all clearly part of Baba Yaga's curse."
speaker: guard

text: "The Baronet is busy hibernating."
speaker: guard

text: "The Baronet Barnard von Spielburg was a proud young man with the dark hair and eyes of his father, the Baron."
speaker: guard

text: "He rode off one morning and his horse returned without him.  The horse had been raked by the claws of some large animal.  There is still a reward offered for the safe return of the Baronet, but I fear he is dead."
speaker: guard

text: "Elsa von Spielburg was a lovely young child with braided brown hair and bright blue eyes, like her mother, the Baroness, used to have."
speaker: guard

text: "A large winged creature flew over the castle walls, grabbed Elsa, and vanished before the guards could fire upon it.  We searched everywhere for her, but to no avail."
speaker: guard

text: "Even the jester, Yorick, joined the search for her.  The Baron sent a troup of guards to storm Baba Yaga's hut, but I think their skulls sit atop her fence now."
speaker: guard

text: "Elsa von Spielburg would be about eighteen now."
speaker: guard

text: "The stablekeeper could use a strong young person to help clean out the stable.  It does not pay much, but it is good, honest work.  If you would like to take the job, just ask me to open the portcullis."
speaker: guard

text: "Cleaning up after the horses is a good way to build up your muscles."
speaker: guard

text: "The Baron ordered Baba Yaga to leave and she first cursed the graveyard.  When he then sent the Baronial Guards after her, only the captain returned to the castle."
speaker: guard

text:
"He spoke the words,
   'Hear me, oh mighty Baron.
You have angered me, and thus you are cursed.
All that you value you shall lose;
all that you treasure shall be taken from you.'
The captain then dissolved into a pool of sticky blackness."
speaker: guard

text: "He was a very jolly fool who loved to laugh.  He was devoted to Elsa and swore he would not return until Elsa was safe."
speaker: guard

text: "The powerful Ogress Baba Yaga is the cause of all the Baron's misfortune.  She is a vile creature who knows much magic."
speaker: guard

text: "There is still a reward of 50 gold offered for the safe return of the Baronet and Elsa von Spielburg."
speaker: guard

text: "For the capture or death of the brigand Leader there is the reward of 60 gold and the title of 'Hero of the Realm of Spielburg'."
speaker: guard

text: "The brigands are protected by a magic user.  Not much is known about him, but he seems to use more magic potions and powders than spells."
speaker: guard

text: "The leader of the brigands must be a good strategist.  He has organized and led many raids which result in much treasure and little loss on the part of the brigands.  Without their leader, the brigands would soon flee."
speaker: guard

text: "There are now too few guards to take out the brigands, who still rob our people and drive away the traders.  They hide somewhere near the edge of this valley."
speaker: guard

text: "This was once a very wealthy valley.  Many merchants have been robbed by the brigands.  There is a merchant in town who could tell you about his recent loss."
speaker: guard

text: "Baba Yaga cursed the graveyard such that the dead can not rest at night.  One cannot go near the graveyard at dark without risking one's own death unless one uses protection."
speaker: guard

text: "There is an unguent you can purchase from the Healer which protects against the dead that walk at night."
speaker: guard

text: "'Hero of the Realm of Spielburg' is a title of respect and honor.  A true hero can release this land from its curse by using intelligence and courage."
speaker: guard

text: "There are many foul creatures stalking the forest.  The Baron has too few guards to protect this valley from them."
speaker: guard

text: "Spielburg Valley was once a prosperous and peaceful place before the Baron lost his family.  Now the Baron will not leave his castle and has no idea of the ruin this land has come to."
speaker: guard

text: "Baba Yaga has her hut to the northwest.  It is a very dangerous place and many Baronial Guards were lost there."
speaker: guard

text: "The Baroness died soon after the birth of her daughter.  The Baron still mourns for her."
speaker: guard

text: "The Baron von Spielburg lost most of his guards trying to defeat Baba Yaga."
speaker: guard

text: "The gatehouse is a shelter and resting place for the guards."
speaker: guard

text: "Everybody talks about the weather, but nobody does anything about it."
speaker: guard

text: "The Weapons Master often practices in the castle courtyard.  He gives lessons in the art of swordplay.  The lessons are expensive, but well worth the price."
speaker: guard

text: "I wouldn't know about that, I don't get around much these days."
speaker: guard

text: The castle is securely locked for the night.
speaker: narrator

text: The portcullis has been drawn, and the way to the castle is clear.
speaker: narrator

text: The portcullis presents a severe obstacle for entry to the castle.
speaker: narrator

text: The castle of the Baron Stefan von Spielburg, Lord of the valley.
speaker: narrator

text: The gatehouse is a massive structure which houses the guards.
speaker: narrator

text: The gatehouse is a massive structure.
speaker: narrator

text: The mountains in the distance ring the valley of Spielburg, creating a formidable barrier to travelers.
speaker: narrator

text: It looks like the talking is over.
speaker: narrator

text: "No more talking, my friend.  Let's have fun!"
speaker: warlock

text: Now??
speaker: narrator

text: "ME" sees you preparing your spell and...
speaker: narrator

text: You're too busy.
speaker: narrator

text: "ME" sees you preparing to throw and...
speaker: narrator

text: Why would you want to throw that?
speaker: narrator

text: "You know me?  Then perhaps you have come to help.  Perhaps you are able to take the child from the band."
speaker: warlock

text: "Be more specific, my friend."
speaker: warlock

text: "Talking is a speciality of those who know not what they talk about."
speaker: warlock

text: "You know Elsa?  Then perhaps you have come to help.  Perhaps you are able to take the child from the band."
speaker: warlock

text: After you have explained what you know about Elsa, Yorick speaks:
speaker: narrator

text: "It's about time that someone else helped Elsa before it's too late!  Toro and I are the only friends she's got here."
speaker: warlock

text: "She's a lot like her dad, though.  She organized these brigands and changed them from mere thugs to major raiders!"
speaker: warlock

text: "She plans to move the entire band away from the valley as soon as the snow clears.  If that happens, I know she will never be disenchanted."
speaker: warlock

text: "You know about the enchantment?  Then perhaps you have come to help.  Perhaps you are able to take the child from the band."
speaker: warlock

text: "Elsa doesn't know who she really is.  That's part of the enchantment."
speaker: warlock

text: "When Elsa was left with some brigands ten years ago, all her memories were taken away from her."
speaker: warlock

text: "I borrowed a Magic Mirror from a friend to try to break the spell, but it only works when spells are cast directly at you."
speaker: warlock

text: "If you're going to be the big Hero, I hope you brought a Dispel Potion or something."
speaker: warlock

text: "Alas, poor Yorick!  I know me well."
speaker: warlock

text: "Ten years ago, I swore I would find Elsa no matter what.  It took me two years to find her, here with the brigands, and I've posed as her warlock ever since."
speaker: warlock

text: "I couldn't break the spell on her, nor could I leave her to grow up into an attractive woman surrounded by rogues and scoundrels."
speaker: warlock

text: "Someone had to defend her honor!"
speaker: warlock

text: "Can't you see that I am he?  I try to inspire (before they expire) a sense of awe and majesty in those that enter here."
speaker: warlock

text: "After all, I am the Brigand Warlock.  One has to keep up one's appearances, or appear to keep up with one's peers."
speaker: warlock

text: "Everybody needs to know a few magic tricks to get by these days.  You'd be surprised how effective Sleeping Powder is for taking out the guards of a caravan."
speaker: warlock

text: "You snooze, you lose.  And my banana trick has appeal of its own."
speaker: warlock

text: "For thirty years I was the Court Jester.  Now I jest enjoy watching other people take the pratfalls."
speaker: warlock

text: "I'll protect the Brigand Leader with everything I've got.  That's no laughing matter as I get madder."
speaker: warlock

text: "The true Dispel Potion will break an enchantment.  You must splash the potion on Elsa as soon as you can, or she'll skewer you for sure.  She's more than a match for old "Swordy Lordy" now!"
speaker: warlock

text: "Of which potion do you speak?"
speaker: warlock

text: "You must know the "Blade Braggart".  He used to show off in the castle courtyard all the time."
speaker: warlock

text: "Elsa once asked if "Rapier Ruler" would teach her to use the sword, and the "Sword Bore" told her he would not waste his talent on females."
speaker: warlock

text: "She came to me in tears.  After all, she was only nine at the time."
speaker: warlock

text: "She can teach old "Weapon Windbag" a thing or two now!  Her arm is true, and she's truly disarming."
speaker: warlock

text: "There are plenty of doors.  You wouldn't want me to spoil your fun, now!"
speaker: warlock

text: "Either you'll figure it out in the end, or you'll end it before you're out."
speaker: warlock

text: "This room is my way of keeping the brigands from bothering the Leader about raises, since it tends to raze the brigands."
speaker: warlock

text: "I designed it myself!  This place has people rolling in the aisles."
speaker: warlock

text: "Ah yes, the "Mirror with the Leer", as Erasmus calls it (or was it Fenrus?).  It reflects a spell back on the caster of the spell."
speaker: warlock

text:
The Warlock ruminates:
"Now where did I put that mirror...
was it on the desk in Elsa's office?"
say: Now where did I put that mirror... was it on the desk in Elsa's office?
speaker: warlock

text: "Toro is the minotaur that guards this fortress.  Elsa found him when he was just a calf, and he's kowtowed to her ever since."
speaker: warlock

text: He guarantees that the brigands won't give Elsa any bull."
speaker: warlock

text: "This fort is the brigand's palace.  It has been well-constructed."
speaker: warlock
